text.NRSV: When Saul saw David go out against the Philistine, he said to Abner, the commander of the army, “Abner, whose son is this young man?” Abner said, “As your soul lives, O king, I do not know.”
text.Rotherham: And, when Saul saw David going forth to meet the Philistine, he said unto Abner, prince of the host, Whose son is the young man, Abner? And Abner said, By the life of thy soul, O king! I know not.

text.NRSV: The king said, “Inquire whose son the stripling is.”
text.Rotherham: Then said the king,—Ask, thou, whose son the stripling is?

text.NRSV: On David’s return from killing the Philistine, Abner took him and brought him before Saul, with the head of the Philistine in his hand.
text.Rotherham: And, when David returned from smiting the Philistine, Abner took him, and brought him before Saul,—with the head of the Philistine in his hand.

text.NRSV: Saul said to him, “Whose son are you, young man?” David answered, “I am the son of your servant Jesse the Bethlehemite.”
text.Rotherham: And Saul said unto him, Whose son art, thou, O young man? And David said, Son of thy servant Jesse, the Bethlehemite.

text.NRSV: When David had finished speaking to Saul, the soul of Jonathan was bound to the soul of David, and Jonathan loved him as his own soul.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, when he had made an end of speaking unto Saul, that, the soul of Jonathan, was knit with, the soul of David, so that Jonathan loved him, as his own soul.

text.NRSV: Saul took him that day and would not let him return to his father’s house.
text.Rotherham: And Saul took him, that day,—and suffered him not to return unto the house of his father.

text.NRSV: Then Jonathan made a covenant with David, because he loved him as his own soul.
text.Rotherham: And Jonathan and David solemnised a covenant,—because he loved him as his own soul.

text.NRSV: Jonathan stripped himself of the robe that he was wearing, and gave it to David, and his armor, and even his sword and his bow and his belt.
text.Rotherham: And Jonathan stripped himself of the robe which was upon him, and gave it to David,—and his equipments, even to his sword, and to his bow, and to his girdle.

text.NRSV: David went out and was successful wherever Saul sent him; so that Saul made him a commander of the men of war. And this was agreeable to all the people and also to Saul’s servants.
text.Rotherham: And David went forth—whithersoever Saul sent him, he behaved himself prudently, so Saul set him over the men of war,—and he became a favourite in the eyes of all the people, yea even in the eyes of the servants of Saul.

text.NRSV: As they were coming home, when David returned from defeating the Philistine, the women came out of all the towns of Israel, singing and dancing, to meet King Saul, with tambourines, with songs of joy, and with instruments of music.
text.Rotherham: But so it was, when they came in on the return of David from the smiting of the Philistine, that the women went forth out of all the cities of Israel, singing and dancing, to meet Saul the king,—with timbrels, with rejoicing, and with instruments of three strings.

text.NRSV: And the women sang to one another as they made merry, “Saul has killed his thousands, and David his ten thousands.”
text.Rotherham: And the women that made merry responded to each other in song, and said,—Saul, hath smitten, his thousands, but, David, his, tens of thousands.

text.NRSV: Saul was very angry, for this saying displeased him. He said, “They have ascribed to David ten thousands, and to me they have ascribed thousands; what more can he have but the kingdom?”
text.Rotherham: Then was Saul exceeding angry, and this saying was offensive in his eyes, and he said,—They have ascribed, to David, ten thousands, but, to me, have they ascribed thousands,—What, more, then, can he have but, the kingdom?

text.NRSV: So Saul eyed David from that day on.
text.Rotherham: And Saul was eyeing David from that day forward.

text.NRSV: The next day an evil spirit from God rushed upon Saul, and he raved within his house, while David was playing the lyre, as he did day by day. Saul had his spear in his hand;
text.Rotherham: And, when it came to pass, on the morrow, that a superhuman spirit of sadness came suddenly upon Saul, and he was moved to raving in the midst of the house,—and, David, began playing with his hand, as he had done day by day, that a spear being in Saul’s hand,

text.NRSV: and Saul threw the spear, for he thought, “I will pin David to the wall.” But David eluded him twice.
text.Rotherham: Saul hurled the spear, and said to himself—I will smite David, even to the wall! But David moved round from before him, twice.

text.NRSV: Saul was afraid of David, because the Lord was with him but had departed from Saul.
text.Rotherham: And Saul feared because of David,—for Yahweh was with him, whereas, from Saul, he had departed.

text.NRSV: So Saul removed him from his presence, and made him a commander of a thousand; and he marched out and came in, leading the army.
text.Rotherham: So Saul removed him from him, and appointed him to be for him the captain of a thousand,—and he went out and came in before the people.

text.NRSV: David had success in all his undertakings; for the Lord was with him.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass that David, in all his ways, acted prudently,—and, Yahweh, was with him.

text.NRSV: When Saul saw that he had great success, he stood in awe of him.
text.Rotherham: And, when Saul saw that, he, was acting very prudently, he was afraid of him.

text.NRSV: All Israel and Judah loved David; for he went out and came in before them.
text.Rotherham: But, all Israel and Judah, were in love with David,—for he was going out and coming in before them.

text.NRSV: Saul said to David, “Here is my elder daughter Merab; I will give her to you as wife; only be valiant for me and fight the Lord’s battles.” For Saul thought, “Let not my hand be against him, but let the hand of the Philistines be against him.”
text.Rotherham: So then Saul said unto David—Lo! my elder daughter Merab, her, will I give thee to wife, only, approve thyself unto me as a son of valour and fight the battles of Yahweh. Saul, however, had said to himself—Let not, my own hand, be upon him, but let, the hand of the Philistines, be upon him.

text.NRSV: David said to Saul, “Who am I, and what is my life or my family in Israel, that I should become the king’s son-in-law?”
text.Rotherham: And David said unto Saul—Who am, I, or who are my kinsfolk, the family of my father, in Israel,—that I should become son-in-law, to the king?

text.NRSV: But at the time when Merab, Saul’s daughter, should have been given to David, she was given to Adriel the Meholathite as wife.
text.Rotherham: But it came to pass, within the time for giving Merab daughter of Saul to David, that, she, was given to Adriel the Meholathite, to wife.

text.NRSV: Now Saul’s daughter Michal loved David. Saul was told, and the thing pleased him.
text.Rotherham: Then did Michal, Saul’s daughter, love David,—and it was told Saul, and the thing was right in his eyes.

text.NRSV: Saul thought, “Let me give her to him that she may be a snare for him, and that the hand of the Philistines may be against him.” Therefore Saul said to David a second time, “You shall now be my son-in-law.”
text.Rotherham: And Saul said to himself—I will give her unto him, that she may prove to him a snare, and that, the hand of the Philistines, may be upon him. So then Saul said unto David, A second time, mayest thou become my son-in-law to-day.

text.NRSV: Saul commanded his servants, “Speak to David in private and say, ‘See! The king is delighted with you, and all his servants love you; now then become the king’s son-in-law.’”
text.Rotherham: And Saul commanded his servants—Speak ye unto David quietly saying, Lo! the king delighteth in thee, and, all his servants, love thee,—now, therefore, become thou son-in-law to the king.

text.NRSV: So Saul’s servants spoke those words in the ears of David. David said, “Does it seem to you a little thing to become the king’s son-in-law, seeing that I am a poor man and of little account?”
text.Rotherham: And the servants of Saul spake, in the ears of David, these words. And David said—Seemeth it a light thing, in your eyes, to become son-in-law to the king, seeing that, I, am a poor man and lightly esteemed?

text.NRSV: Then Saul’s servants told him, “This is what David said.”
text.Rotherham: So the servants of Saul told him, saying,—According to these words, spake David,

text.NRSV: Then Saul said, “Thus shall you say to David, ‘The king desires no marriage present except a hundred foreskins of the Philistines, that he may be avenged on the king’s enemies.’” Now Saul planned to make David fall by the hand of the Philistines.
text.Rotherham: Then said Saul—Thus, shall ye say unto David—The king hath no delight in purchase-price, but rather in a hundred foreskins of Philistines, by avenging himself on the enemies of the king. But, Saul, thought to let David fall by the hand of the Philistines.

text.NRSV: When his servants told David these words, David was pleased to be the king’s son-in-law. Before the days had expired,
text.Rotherham: So, when his servants told David these words, the thing was right in the eyes of David, to become son-in-law unto the king,—and the days had not expired.

text.NRSV: David rose and went, along with his men, and killed one hundred of the Philistines, and brought their foreskins, which were given in full number to the king, that he might become the king’s son-in-law. Saul gave him his daughter Michal as a wife.
text.Rotherham: Wherefore David arose, and went—he and his men, and smote among the Philistines two hundred men, and David brought in their foreskins, and gave them in full tale unto the king, that he might become son-in-law unto the king, and Saul gave him Michal his daughter, to wife.

text.NRSV: But when Saul realized that the Lord was with David, and that Saul’s daughter Michal loved him,
text.Rotherham: And Saul saw and knew that, Yahweh, was with David, and that, all Israel, loved him.

text.NRSV: Saul was still more afraid of David. So Saul was an enemy of David from that day forward.
text.Rotherham: So then Saul went on to fear because of David, yet more,—and it came to pass that Saul was hostile to David, all the days.

text.NRSV: Then the commanders of the Philistines came out to battle; and as often as they came out, David had more success than all the servants of Saul; so his name was highly esteemed.
text.Rotherham: And, when the princes of the Philistines came forth, so it was, that, as often as they came forth, David was more circumspect than any of the servants of Saul, so that his name was, precious exceedingly.

text.NRSV: Saul spoke to his son Jonathan and to all his servants about killing David. But Saul’s son Jonathan took great delight in David.
text.Rotherham: Then spake Saul unto Jonathan his son, and unto all his servants, that they should put David to death;

text.NRSV: Jonathan told David, “My father Saul is trying to kill you; therefore be on guard tomorrow morning; stay in a secret place and hide yourself.
text.Rotherham: but, Jonathan, Saul’s son, delighted in David, exceedingly,—so Jonathan told David, saying, Saul, my father, is seeking to put thee to death,—now, therefore, take heed to thyself I pray thee, in the morning, and abide thou in concealment, and hide thyself;

text.NRSV: I will go out and stand beside my father in the field where you are, and I will speak to my father about you; if I find out anything, I will tell you.”
text.Rotherham: and, I, will come out and stand beside my father, in the field where, thou, art, and, I, will speak of thee unto my father,—and, if I see aught, I will tell thee.

text.NRSV: Jonathan spoke well of David to his father Saul, and said to him, “The king should not sin against his servant David, because he has not sinned against you, and because his deeds have been of good service to you;
text.Rotherham: So then Jonathan spake of David things that were good, unto Saul his father,—and said unto him—May the king not sin against his servant—against David; for he hath not sinned against, thee, nay, indeed, his doings have been good to theeward exceedingly.

text.NRSV: for he took his life in his hand and attacked the Philistine, and the Lord brought about a great victory for all Israel. You saw it, and rejoiced; why then will you sin against innocent blood by killing David without cause?”
text.Rotherham: And, when he put his life in his hand, and smote the Philistine, and Yahweh wrought a great victory for all Israel, thou sawest, and didst rejoice. Wherefore, then, shouldst thou sin against innocent blood by putting David to death, without cause?

text.NRSV: Saul heeded the voice of Jonathan; Saul swore, “As the Lord lives, he shall not be put to death.”
text.Rotherham: And Saul hearkened unto the voice of Jonathan,—and Saul sware, By the life of Yahweh, he shall not be put to death!

text.NRSV: So Jonathan called David, and told him all these things. Then Jonathan brought David to Saul, and he was in his presence as before.
text.Rotherham: And Jonathan called for David, and Jonathan told him all these things,—and Jonathan brought in David unto Saul, and so he was in his presence, as aforetime.

text.NRSV: Once again war broke out, and David went out and fought with the Philistines. He struck them down with great force, and they fled before him.
text.Rotherham: But, when the war again brake out,—and David went forth and fought with the Philistines, and smote them with a great smiting, and they fled before him,

text.NRSV: Then an evil spirit from the Lord came upon Saul, as he sat in his house with his spear in his hand. David was playing music.
text.Rotherham: then came there a sad spirit of Yahweh unto Saul, he being in his house, seated, with his spear in his hand,—while, David, played with his hand,

text.NRSV: Saul tried to pin David to the wall with the spear; but he eluded Saul, so that he struck the spear into the wall. David fled and escaped that night.
text.Rotherham: Saul sought to smite David with the spear, even to the wall, but he slipped away from before Saul, who smote the spear into the wall,—whereas, David, fled and escaped, that night.

text.NRSV: Saul sent messengers to David’s house to watch him, in order to kill him in the morning. But David’s wife Michal told him, “If you do not save your life tonight, tomorrow you will be killed.”
text.Rotherham: And Saul sent messengers unto David’s house, to watch him, and to put him to death, in the morning! And Michal his wife told David, saying, If thou do not deliver thyself to-night, to-morrow, art thou to be put to death.

text.NRSV: So Michal let David down through the window; he fled away and escaped.
text.Rotherham: So Michal let down David, through the window, and he departed and fled, and escaped.

text.NRSV: Michal took an idol and laid it on the bed; she put a net of goats’ hair over its head, and covered it with the clothes.
text.Rotherham: And Michal took the household god, and put it in the bed, and, a fly-net of goat’s-hair, put she at its head,—and covered it with the clothes.

text.NRSV: When Saul sent messengers to take David, she said, “He is sick.”
text.Rotherham: And, when Saul sent messengers to take David, she said—He is, sick.

text.NRSV: Then Saul sent the messengers to see David for themselves. He said, “Bring him up to me in the bed, that I may kill him.”
text.Rotherham: So Saul sent messengers to see David, saying,—Bring him up in the bed unto me, that I may put him to death.

text.NRSV: When the messengers came in, there was the idol in the bed, with the cover of goats’ hair over its head.
text.Rotherham: And, when the messengers entered, lo! the household god in the bed,—with a fly-net of goat’s-hair at its head.

text.NRSV: Saul said to Michal, “Why have you deceived me like this, and let my enemy go, so that he has escaped?” Michal answered Saul, “He said to me, ‘Let me go; why should I kill you?’”
text.Rotherham: And Saul said unto Michal—Wherefore, in this way, hast thou deceived me, and let go mine enemy, that he hath escaped? Then said Michal unto Saul, He, himself, said unto me—Let me go, wherefore should I put thee to death?

text.NRSV: Now David fled and escaped; he came to Samuel at Ramah, and told him all that Saul had done to him. He and Samuel went and lived in Naioth.
text.Rotherham: So, David, fled, and escaped, and came in unto Samuel in Ramah, and told him all that Saul had done to him,—and he and Samuel departed, and dwelt in Naioth.

text.NRSV: Saul was told, “David is at Naioth in Ramah.”
text.Rotherham: And it was told Saul, saying,—Lo! David, is in Naioth, in Ramah.

text.NRSV: Then Saul sent messengers to take David. When they saw the company of the prophets in a frenzy, with Samuel standing in charge of them, the spirit of God came upon the messengers of Saul, and they also prophesied.
text.Rotherham: So Saul sent messengers to take David, but, when they saw the assembly of prophets prophesying, and Samuel standing as head over them, then came the Spirit of God upon the messengers of Saul, and, they also, were, moved to prophesy.

text.NRSV: Saul was told, and he sent other messengers, and they also prophesied. So Saul sent messengers again the third time, and they also prophesied.
text.Rotherham: And, when they told Saul, and he sent other messengers, then were, they also, moved to prophesy. And, when Saul again sent messengers a third time, then were, they also, moved to prophesy.

text.NRSV: Then he himself went to Ramah. He came to the great well that is in Secu; he asked, “Where are Samuel and David?” Someone said, “They are at Naioth in Ramah.”
text.Rotherham: Then went, he also himself, to Ramah, and came in as far as the well of the threshing-floor which is in Sephi, and asked and said, Where are Samuel and David? And one said—Lo! in Naioth, in Ramah.

text.NRSV: He went there, toward Naioth in Ramah; and the spirit of God came upon him also, and he went on, and prophesied, until he came to Naioth in Ramah.
text.Rotherham: And, when he departed from thence towards Naioth in Ramah, then came upon, him also, the Spirit of God, and he went on and on, and was moved to prophesy, until he entered Naioth in Ramah.

text.NRSV: He too stripped off his clothes, and he too prophesied before Samuel. He lay naked all that day and all that night. That is why they say, “Is Saul also among the prophets?”
text.Rotherham: And, even he, stripped off his upper garments, and, even he, was moved to prophesy before Samuel, and lay prostrate, disrobed, all that day, and all the night. For this cause, do they say, Is, even Saul, among the prophets?

text.NRSV: David fled from Naioth in Ramah. He came before Jonathan and said, “What have I done? What is my guilt? And what is my sin against your father, that he is trying to take my life?”
text.Rotherham: And David fled from Naioth, in Ramah,—and came in, and said before Jonathan—What have I done? What is my transgression, and what my sin, before thy father, that he seeketh my life?

text.NRSV: He said to him, “Far from it! You shall not die. My father does nothing either great or small without disclosing it to me; and why should my father hide this from me? It is not so!”
text.Rotherham: And he said unto him—Far be it! thou shalt not die. Lo! my father doeth nothing, great or small, without unveiling mine ear,—wherefore, then, should my father hide from me, this thing? There is, nothing, in this.

text.NRSV: But David also swore, “Your father knows very well that you like me; and he has said, ‘Jonathan must not know this, or he will be grieved.’ But truly, as the Lord lives and as you yourself live, there is but a step between me and death.”
text.Rotherham: But David sware yet further, and said—Thy father, doth know, that I have found favour in thine eyes, therefore saith he,—Do not let Jonathan know this, lest he grieve; but, indeed, by the life of Yahweh, and by the life of thine own soul, surely there is but as it were a step betwixt me and death!

text.NRSV: Then Jonathan said to David, “Whatever you say, I will do for you.”
text.Rotherham: And Jonathan said unto David,—Whatsoever thy soul shall desire, I will do for thee.

text.NRSV: David said to Jonathan, “Tomorrow is the new moon, and I should not fail to sit with the king at the meal; but let me go, that I may hide in the field until the third day at evening.
text.Rotherham: And David said unto Jonathan—Lo! the new moon, is to-morrow, and, I, must not sit with the king, to eat,—let me go then, and hide myself in the field, until the evening.

text.NRSV: If your father misses me at all, then say, ‘David earnestly asked leave of me to hurry to Bethlehem his home town; for there is a yearly sacrifice there for all the family.’
text.Rotherham: If thy father, enquire, for me, then shalt thou say—David, did ask leave, of me, to run to Bethlehem, his own city, for, a yearly sacrifice, [is to be held] there for all the family.

text.NRSV: If he says, ‘Very well,’ your servant shall be safe; but if he is very angry, then know that evil has been determined by him.
text.Rotherham: If, thus, he say—It is well,—thy servant shall have, peace,—but, if it, anger, him, know that harm hath been determined by him.

text.NRSV: Therefore deal kindly with your servant, for you have brought your servant into a sacred covenant with you. But if there is guilt in me, kill me yourself! Why should you bring me to your father?”
text.Rotherham: Thus shalt thou do a lovingkindness for thy servant, for, into a covenant of Yahweh, hast thou brought thy servant, with thee,—But, if there is in me transgression, put me to death, thyself, for, unto thy father, wherefore shouldst thou bring me in?

text.NRSV: Jonathan said, “Far be it from you! If I knew that it was determined by my father that evil should come upon you, would I not tell you?”
text.Rotherham: And Jonathan said—Far be it from thee! but, if I, get to know, that harm is determined by my father, to bring it upon thee, is not, that, the thing that I will tell thee?

text.NRSV: Then David said to Jonathan, “Who will tell me if perchance your father answers you harshly?”
text.Rotherham: Then said David unto Jonathan, Who shall tell me,—if thy father answer thee aught that is, harsh?

text.NRSV: Jonathan said to David, “Come, let us go out into the field.” So they both went out into the field.
text.Rotherham: And Jonathan said unto David, Come! and let us go out into the field. And they two went out into the field.

text.NRSV: Jonathan said to him, “By the Lord, the God of Israel! When I have sounded my father about this time tomorrow, or the third day, if he is well disposed toward David, shall I not then send and disclose it to you?
text.Rotherham: Then said Jonathan unto David—Witness [be] Yahweh God of Israel, that I will sound my father about this time to-morrow or the third day, and lo! if there he good towards David, will I not, then, send unto thee, and unveil thine ear?

text.NRSV: But if it pleases my father to do you harm, the Lord do so to Jonathan, and more also, if I do not disclose it to you, and send you away, so that you may go in safety. May the Lord be with you, as he has been with my father.
text.Rotherham: So, let Yahweh do unto Jonathan, and, so, let him add—when harm against thee seemeth good unto my father, then will I unveil thine ear, and let thee go, and thou shall depart in peace,—then Yahweh be with thee, as he hath been with my father.

text.NRSV: If I am still alive, show me the faithful love of the Lord, so that I may not die,
text.Rotherham: And, not only while I yet live, shalt thou deal with me in the lovingkindness of Yahweh, that I die not:

text.NRSV: and do not cut off your loyalty from my house forever. When the Lord cuts off every one of the enemies of David from the face of the earth,
text.Rotherham: but thou shalt not cut off thy lovingkindness from my house, unto times age-abiding,—no! not when Yahweh hath cut off the enemies of David, every one from off the face of the ground.

text.NRSV: then the name of Jonathan shall not be cut off from the house of David. May the Lord seek out the enemies of David.
text.Rotherham: Thus Jonathan solemnised a covenant with the house of David [saying],—So let Yahweh require it, at the hand of the enemies of David.

text.NRSV: Jonathan made David swear again by his love for him; for he loved him as he loved his own life.
text.Rotherham: And again Jonathan sware unto David, by his love to him,—for, as he loved his own soul, so loved he him.

text.NRSV: Jonathan said to him, “Tomorrow is the new moon, and you will be missed, because your seat will be empty.
text.Rotherham: Then said Jonathan unto him—To-morrow, is the new moon, and thou wilt be missed, for thy seat will be empty;

text.NRSV: When you have stayed three days, you shall go down quickly and come to the place where you hid yourself earlier, and shall remain beside the stone Ezel.
text.Rotherham: and, when thou hast tarried three days, thou shalt come down quickly and enter the place, where thou didst hide thyself on the day of the deed, and shall remain by the side of this mound.

text.NRSV: I will shoot three arrows to the side of it, as though I shot at a mark.
text.Rotherham: And, as for me—three arrows to the side, will I shoot,—as though I shot at a mark.

text.NRSV: Then I will send the boy, saying, ‘Go, find the arrows.’ If I say to the boy, ‘Look, the arrows are on this side of you, collect them,’ then come, for, as the Lord lives, you are safe; there is no danger.
text.Rotherham: And lo! I will send the boy [saying], Go, find the arrow! If I, say, to the boy—Lo! the arrows, are on this side of thee—take them, then come, for there is peace for thee, and no cause [for fear], by the life of Yahweh.

text.NRSV: But if I say to the young man, ‘Look, the arrows are beyond you,’ then go your way; for the Lord has sent you away.
text.Rotherham: But, if, thus, I say to the youth, Lo! the arrows are beyond thee, depart, for Yahweh hath sent thee away.

text.NRSV: As for the matter that you and I have spoken of, the Lord is witness between you and me forever.”
text.Rotherham: But, as touching the matter whereof we spake—I and thou, lo! Yahweh, be betwixt me and thee, unto times age-abiding.

text.NRSV: So David hid himself in the field. When the new moon came, the king sat down to eat food.
text.Rotherham: So David hid himself in the field—and, when the new moon had come, the king sat down to eat, food,

text.NRSV: The king sat upon his seat, as at other times, upon the seat by the wall. Jonathan stood, while Abner sat by Saul’s side; but David’s place was empty.
text.Rotherham: yea the king sat down on his seat, as at other times, by the seat against the wall, and, when Jonathan arose, Abner seated himself by the side of Saul,—but David’s place was empty.

text.NRSV: Saul did not say anything that day; for he thought, “Something has befallen him; he is not clean—surely he is not clean.”
text.Rotherham: Saul, however, spake nothing that day,—for he said to himself—It is, an accident, he is, not clean, because he hath not been cleansed.

text.NRSV: The next day after the new moon, the second day, David’s place was empty. Saul said to his son Jonathan, “Why has the son of Jesse not come to the feast, either yesterday or today?”
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, on the morrow, the second of the month, that David’s place was empty,—and Saul said unto Jonathan his son, Wherefore hath not the son of Jesse come in, either yesterday or to-day, to meat?

text.NRSV: Jonathan answered Saul, “David earnestly asked leave of me to hurry to Bethlehem his home town;
text.Rotherham: And Jonathan answered Saul,—David, asked leave, of me, [to go] as far as Bethlehem;

text.NRSV: he said, ‘Let me go, for our family is holding a sacrifice in the city; and my brother has commanded me to be there. So now, if I have found favor in your sight, let me get away, and see my brothers.’ For this reason he has not come to the king’s table.”
text.Rotherham: and said—Let me go, I pray thee, for, a family sacrifice, have we in the city, and my brethren have commanded me, now, therefore, if I have found favour in thine eyes, let me slip away, I pray thee, that I may see my brethren. For this cause, hath he not come in unto the table of the king.

text.NRSV: Then Saul’s anger was kindled against Jonathan. He said to him, “You son of a perverse, rebellious woman! Do I not know that you have chosen the son of Jesse to your own shame, and to the shame of your mother’s nakedness?
text.Rotherham: Then was Saul’s anger kindled against Jonathan, and he said unto him, Thou son of rebellious perversity! do I not know that thou art, confederate, with the son of Jesse to thine own confusion, and to the confusion of the shame of thy mother?

text.NRSV: For as long as the son of Jesse lives upon the earth, neither you nor your kingdom shall be established. Now send and bring him to me, for he shall surely die.”
text.Rotherham: For, as long as, the son of Jesse, liveth on the ground, thou wilt not be established, thou nor thy kingdom, Now, therefore, send and fetch him unto me, for, doomed to death, is he!

text.NRSV: Jonathan answered his father Saul, “Why should he be put to death? What has he done?”
text.Rotherham: And Jonathan responded to Saul his father,—and said unto him—Wherefore must he be put to death, What hath he done?

text.NRSV: But Saul threw his spear at Jonathan, to strike him; so Jonathan knew that it was the decision of his father to put David to death.
text.Rotherham: And Saul hurled his spear at him, to smite him. So Jonathan knew that it was, determined, of his father, to put David to death.

text.NRSV: In the morning Jonathan went out into the field to the appointed place with David, and with him a little boy.
text.Rotherham: Jonathan therefore rose up from the table, in a glow of anger,—and did eat no food on the second day of the month, for he was grieved for David, because his father had reviled him.

text.NRSV: He said to the boy, “Run and find the arrows that I shoot.” As the boy ran, he shot an arrow beyond him.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass in the morning, that Jonathan went forth into the field, to the place appointed with David,—a little lad, being with him.

text.NRSV: When the boy came to the place where Jonathan’s arrow had fallen, Jonathan shouted after the boy and said, “Is the arrow not beyond you?”
text.Rotherham: And he said to his lad—Run, find, I pray thee, the arrows which I am about to shoot. The boy, ran, but, he, shot the arrow beyond him.

text.NRSV: Jonathan shouted after the boy, “Hurry, be quick, do not delay!” So Jonathan’s boy gathered up the arrows and came to his master.
text.Rotherham: And, when the lad came as far as the place of the arrow which Jonathan had shot, Jonathan cried after the lad, and said, Is not the arrow, beyond, thee?

text.NRSV: Jonathan said to the boy, “Take the arrows,” and he took them. He gave his weapons to the boy and said to him, “Go and carry them to the city.”
text.Rotherham: And Jonathan cried after the lad, Haste thee—be quick, do not stay! so Jonathan’s lad picked up the arrows and brought [them] unto his master.

text.NRSV: As soon as the boy had gone, David rose from beside the stone heap and prostrated himself with his face to the ground. He bowed three times, and they kissed each other, and wept with each other; David wept the more.
text.Rotherham: But, the lad, knew, nothing, only Jonathan and David, knew the matter.

text.NRSV: Jonathan said to David, “Go in peace, for the two of us have sworn in the name of the Lord, saying, ‘The Lord shall be between me and you, and between my descendants and your descendants, forever.’” He rose and departed; and Jonathan went into the city.
text.Rotherham: So Jonathan gave his weapons unto his lad, and said to him, Go carry them into the city.

text.NRSV: As soon as the boy had gone, David rose from beside the stone heap and prostrated himself with his face to the ground. He bowed three times, and they kissed each other, and wept with each other; David wept the more.
text.Rotherham: As soon as, the lad, was gone, David, arose from beside the South, and fell on his face to the earth, and bowed himself down three times,—and they kissed each other, and wept over each other, until, David, exceeded.

text.NRSV: Then Jonathan said to David, "Go in peace, since both of us have sworn in the name of the Lord, saying, "The Lord shall be between me and you, and between my descendants and your descendants, forever.' " He got up and left; and Jonathan went into the city.
text.Rotherham: Then said Jonathan unto David—Go in peace,—[remembering] that, we, have both sworn in the name of Yahweh, saying, Yahweh shall be between me and thee, and between my seed and thy seed, unto times age-abiding. And he arose, and departed,—and, Jonathan, entered the city.

text.NRSV: David came to Nob to the priest Ahimelech. Ahimelech came trembling to meet David, and said to him, “Why are you alone, and no one with you?”
text.Rotherham: Then came David to Nob, unto Ahimelech, the priest,—and Ahimelech trembled when he met David, and said unto him—Why art thou, alone, and, no man, with thee?

text.NRSV: David said to the priest Ahimelech, “The king has commanded me a matter, and said to me, ‘No one must know anything of the matter about which I send you, and with which I have charged you.’ I have directed the young men to such and such a place.
text.Rotherham: And David said unto Ahimelech the priest—The king, hath charged me with a matter, and hath said unto me—Let, no man, know aught of the business on which I am sending thee, and with which I have charged thee,—But, unto the young men, have I appointed, such and such a place.

text.NRSV: Now then, what have you at hand? Give me five loaves of bread, or whatever is here.”
text.Rotherham: Now, therefore, what is there under thy hand? Five loaves, give thou into my hand,—or, whatever can be found.

text.NRSV: The priest answered David, “I have no ordinary bread at hand, only holy bread—provided that the young men have kept themselves from women.”
text.Rotherham: And the priest answered David, and said, There is no common bread under my hand,—but, hallowed bread, there is, if the young men have kept themselves, at least from women.

text.NRSV: David answered the priest, “Indeed women have been kept from us as always when I go on an expedition; the vessels of the young men are holy even when it is a common journey; how much more today will their vessels be holy?”
text.Rotherham: And David answered the priest and said to him—Of a truth, women, have been withheld from us, of late, through my coming out, and the wallets of the young men have become hallowed,—while [the bread], itself, is in a manner common, and the more so since, to-day, [there are other loaves] to be hallowed, in the vessel.

text.NRSV: So the priest gave him the holy bread; for there was no bread there except the bread of the Presence, which is removed from before the Lord, to be replaced by hot bread on the day it is taken away.
text.Rotherham: So the priest gave him hallowed [bread],—because there was there no bread, save the Presence-Bread, which had to be removed from before Yahweh, to put hot bread, on the day when it should be taken away.

text.NRSV: Now a certain man of the servants of Saul was there that day, detained before the Lord; his name was Doeg the Edomite, the chief of Saul’s shepherds.
text.Rotherham: Now, in that very place, was a man of the servants of Saul, on that day, detained before Yahweh, whose name, was Doeg the Edomite,—chief of the shepherds that belonged unto Saul.

text.NRSV: David said to Ahimelech, “Is there here any spear or sword? For I have brought neither my sword nor my weapons with me, because the king’s business was urgent.”
text.Rotherham: Then said David to Ahimelech, See whether there is here, under thy hand, a spear or a sword? for, neither my sword, nor my other weapons, did I take in my hand, for, the king’s business, was, urgent.

text.NRSV: The priest answered, “The sword of Goliath the Philistine, whom you killed in the valley of Elah, is here wrapped in a cloth behind the ephod; if you will take that, take it, for there is no other here except that one.” David said, “There is none like it; give it to me.”
text.Rotherham: And the priest said: The sword of Goliath the Philistine, whom thou didst smite in the vale of Elah, lo! that, is wrapped up in a cloth, behind the ephod, if, that, thou wilt take to thee, take it, for there is no other, save that, here. And David said—There is none, like it, give it me.

text.NRSV: David rose and fled that day from Saul; he went to King Achish of Gath.
text.Rotherham: Then arose David, and fled, that day, from the face of Saul,—and came in unto Achish, king of Gath.

text.NRSV: The servants of Achish said to him, “Is this not David the king of the land? Did they not sing to one another of him in dances, ‘Saul has killed his thousands, and David his ten thousands’?”
text.Rotherham: And the servants of Achish said unto him, Is not, this, David, king of the land? Was it not, of this man, that they kept responding in the dances, saying, Saul, hath smitten his, thousands, but, David, his, tens of thousands?

text.NRSV: David took these words to heart and was very much afraid of King Achish of Gath.
text.Rotherham: And David laid up these words in his heart,—and feared greatly, because of Achish king of Gath.

text.NRSV: So he changed his behavior before them; he pretended to be mad when in their hands. He scratched on the doors of the gate, and let his spittle run down his beard.
text.Rotherham: So he feigned himself mad, in their sight, and raged in their hand,—and struck against the doors of the gate, and let his spittle run down upon his beard.

text.NRSV: Achish said to his servants, “Look, you see the man is mad; why then have you brought him to me?
text.Rotherham: Then said Achish unto his servants,—Lo! ye can see, a madman playing his pranks, wherefore should ye bring him in, unto me?

text.NRSV: Do I lack madmen, that you have brought this fellow to behave as a madman in my presence? Shall this fellow come into my house?”
text.Rotherham: Lacking of madmen, am I that ye should bring in this one to play his mad pranks, unto me? Shall, this, one enter my household?

text.NRSV: David left Gath and escaped to the cave of Adullam; when his brothers and all his father’s house heard it, they went down there to him.
text.Rotherham: David therefore departed thence, and escaped, into the cave of Adullam,—and, when his brethren and all the household of his father heard it, they went down unto him, thither.

text.NRSV: Everyone who was in distress, and everyone who was in debt, and everyone who was discontented gathered to him; and he became captain over them. Those who were with him numbered about four hundred.
text.Rotherham: And there gathered themselves unto him—every one that was in distress, and every one that had a creditor, and every one embittered in soul, and he came to be over them as a prince,—and there were with him, about four hundred men.

text.NRSV: David went from there to Mizpeh of Moab. He said to the king of Moab, “Please let my father and mother come and stay with you, until I know what God will do for me.”
text.Rotherham: And David departed thence, to Mizpah of Moab,—and said unto the king of Moab, I pray thee, let my father and my mother dwell with you, until that I know what God will do for me.

text.NRSV: He left them with the king of Moab, and they stayed with him all the time that David was in the stronghold.
text.Rotherham: So he set them before the king of Moab,—and they dwelt with him, all the days that David was in the fortress.

text.NRSV: Then the prophet Gad said to David, “Do not remain in the stronghold; leave, and go into the land of Judah.” So David left, and went to the forest of Hereth.
text.Rotherham: Then said Gad the prophet unto David—Thou must not abide in the fortress, go and get thee into the land of Judah. So David departed, and entered the forest of Hereth.

text.NRSV: Saul heard that David and those who were with him had been located. Saul was sitting at Gibeah, under the tamarisk tree on the height, with his spear in his hand, and all his servants were standing around him.
text.Rotherham: And, when Saul heard that David was discovered, and the men that were with him—Saul, being seated in Gibeah under the tamarisk-tree in Ramah, with his spear in his hand, and all his servants stationed by him,—

text.NRSV: Saul said to his servants who stood around him, “Hear now, you Benjaminites; will the son of Jesse give every one of you fields and vineyards, will he make every one of you commanders of thousands and commanders of hundreds?
text.Rotherham: then said Saul unto his servants who were stationed by him—Hear, I pray you, ye Benjamites! What! even to all of you, will the son of Jesse give fields and vineyards? All of you, will he appoint to be princes of thousands, and princes of hundreds?

text.NRSV: Is that why all of you have conspired against me? No one discloses to me when my son makes a league with the son of Jesse, none of you is sorry for me or discloses to me that my son has stirred up my servant against me, to lie in wait, as has happened today.”
text.Rotherham: That ye have conspired, all of you, against me, and there is none to uncover mine ear as to the covenanting of my son with the son of Jesse? And none from among you that taketh pity upon me, and that uncovereth mine ear [to this],—that, mine own son, hath stirred up, my servants, against me, to lie in wait, as at this day?

text.NRSV: Then Doeg the Edomite, who was in charge of Saul’s servants, answered, “I saw the son of Jesse coming to Nob, to Ahimelech son of Ahitub;
text.Rotherham: Then answered Doeg the Edomite—he, being stationed near the servants of Saul—and said,—I saw the son of Jesse coming into Nob, unto Ahimelech son of Ahitub;

text.NRSV: he inquired of the Lord for him, gave him provisions, and gave him the sword of Goliath the Philistine.”
text.Rotherham: Then enquired he for him of Yahweh, Provisions also, gave he unto him,—and, the sword of Goliath the Philistine, gave he unto him.

text.NRSV: The king sent someone to summon the priest Ahimelech son of Ahitub and all his father’s house, the priests who were at Nob; all of them came to the king.
text.Rotherham: Then the king sent to call Ahimelech, son of Ahitub, the priest, and all the house of his father—the priests, who were in Nob,—and they came, all of them, unto the king.

text.NRSV: Saul said, “Here now, son of Ahitub.” He answered, “Here I am, my lord.”
text.Rotherham: And Saul said, Hear, I pray thee, thou son of Ahitub! And he said—Behold me! my lord.

text.NRSV: Saul said to him, “Why have you and the son of Jesse conspired against me? Why have you given him bread and a sword, and have inquired of God for him, so that he has risen against me, lying in wait, as has happened today?”
text.Rotherham: And Saul said unto him, Wherefore have ye conspired against me, thou, and the son of Jesse,—in that thou gavest him bread and a sword, and didst enquire for him of God, that he might rise up against me, that he might lie in wait, as at this day?

text.NRSV: Then Ahimelech answered the king, “Who among all your servants is so faithful as David? He is the king’s son-in-law, captain of your bodyguard and respected in your house.
text.Rotherham: Then Ahimelech answered the king, and said,—But who, among all thy servants, like David, is, faithful, being son-in-law to the king, and, cometh near to have audience with thee, and is, honoured in thy household?

text.NRSV: Is today the first time that I have inquired of God for him? By no means! Do not let the king impute anything to his servant or to any member of my father’s house; for your servant knew nothing of all this, much or little.”
text.Rotherham: Did I, that day, begin to enquire for him of God? Far from me! Let not the king impute to his servant such a thing, nor to any of the household of my father, for thy servant knoweth nothing of all this, less or more.

text.NRSV: The king said, “You shall surely die, Ahimelech, you and all your father’s house.”
text.Rotherham: And the king said: Thou shalt, die, Ahimelech,—thou and all the household of thy father.

text.NRSV: The king said to the guard who stood around him, “Turn and kill the priests of the Lord, because their loyalty is with David, and because they knew that he fled, and did not disclose it to me.” But the servants of the king would not raise a hand to attack the priests of the Lord.
text.Rotherham: And the king said unto the runners that were stationed by him—Turn ye round and put to death the priests of Yahweh, because, their hand also, is with David, and because they knew that he was, in flight, and unveiled not mine ear. But the servants of the king were not willing to thrust forth their hand, to fall upon the priests of Yahweh.

text.NRSV: Then the king said to Doeg, “Turn and attack the priests.” Doeg the Edomite turned and attacked the priests; on that day he killed eighty-five who wore the linen ephod.
text.Rotherham: Then said the king to Doeg, Turn, thou, and fall upon the priests. So Doeg the Edomite turned, and, himself, fell upon the priests, and put to death, that day, four score and five men bearing an ephod of linen;

text.NRSV: Nob, the city of the priests, he put to the sword; men and women, children and infants, oxen, donkeys, and sheep, he put to the sword.
text.Rotherham: Nob also, the city of the priests, smote he with the edge of the sword, both men and women, children and sucklings,—and oxen and asses and sheep, with the edge of the sword.

text.NRSV: But one of the sons of Ahimelech son of Ahitub, named Abiathar, escaped and fled after David.
text.Rotherham: But there escaped one son of Ahimelech, son of Ahitub, whose, name, was Abiathar,—and he fled after David.

text.NRSV: Abiathar told David that Saul had killed the priests of the Lord.
text.Rotherham: So Abiathar told David,—that Saul had slain the priests of Yahweh.

text.NRSV: David said to Abiathar, “I knew on that day, when Doeg the Edomite was there, that he would be sure to tell Saul. I am responsible for the lives of all your father’s house.
text.Rotherham: Then said David to Abiathar—I knew, that day, when Doeg the Edomite was, there, that he would, surely tell, Saul. I, am chargeable with all the lives of the house of thy father.

text.NRSV: Stay with me, and do not be afraid; for the one who seeks my life seeks your life; you will be safe with me.”
text.Rotherham: Abide with me! do not fear, for, whoso seeketh my life, seeketh thy life,—for, in safeguard, shall thou be, with me.

text.NRSV: Now they told David, “Look, the Philistines are fighting against Keilah, and are robbing the threshing floors.”
text.Rotherham: Then told they David, saying,—Lo! the Philistines are fighting against Keilah, and they are plundering the threshing-floors.

text.NRSV: David inquired of the Lord, “Shall I go and attack these Philistines?” The Lord answered David, “Go and attack the Philistines and save Keilah.”
text.Rotherham: So David enquired of Yahweh, saying, Shall I go and smite these Philistines? And Yahweh said unto David—Go, and smite the Philistines, and save Keilah.

text.NRSV: But David’s men said to him, “Look, we are already afraid here in Judah; how much more then if we go to Keilah against the armies of the Philistines?”
text.Rotherham: But the men of David said unto him, Lo! we, here, in Judah, are afraid,—how much more, then, if we go to Keilah, against the ranks of the Philistines?

text.NRSV: Then David inquired of the Lord again. The Lord answered him, “Yes, go down to Keilah; for I will give the Philistines into your hand.”
text.Rotherham: So David yet again enquired if Yahweh, and Yahweh answered him,—and said—Arise, go down to Keilah, for I am about to deliver the Philistines into thy hand.

text.NRSV: So David and his men went to Keilah, fought with the Philistines, brought away their livestock, and dealt them a heavy blow. So David rescued the inhabitants of Keilah.
text.Rotherham: David therefore went, with his men, to Keilah, and fought with the Philistines, and drave forth their cattle, and smote them, with a great smiting, so David saved the inhabitants of Keilah.

text.NRSV: When Abiathar son of Ahimelech fled to David at Keilah, he came down with an ephod in his hand.
text.Rotherham: Now it came to pass, when Abiathar, son of Ahimelech, fled unto David to Keilah, that he came down with, an ephod, in his hand.

text.NRSV: Now it was told Saul that David had come to Keilah. And Saul said, “God has given him into my hand; for he has shut himself in by entering a town that has gates and bars.”
text.Rotherham: And, when it was told Saul that David had entered Keilah, Saul said—God hath given him over into my hand, for he hath shut himself in by entering into a city with folding doors and bar.

text.NRSV: Saul summoned all the people for war, to go down to Keilah, to besiege David and his men.
text.Rotherham: So Saul summoned all the people to war,—to go down to Keilah, to besiege David, and his men.

text.NRSV: When David learned that Saul was plotting evil against him, he said to the priest Abiathar, “Bring the ephod here.”
text.Rotherham: And David ascertained that, against him, Saul was contriving mischief,—so he said unto Abiathar the priest, Bring hither the ephod.

text.NRSV: David said, “O Lord, the God of Israel, your servant has heard that Saul seeks to come to Keilah, to destroy the city on my account.
text.Rotherham: Then said David: O Yahweh, God of Israel, thy servant, hath heard, that Saul is seeking to come unto Keilah,—to destroy the city, for my sake:

text.NRSV: And now the citizens of Keilah will surrender me into his hand; will Saul come down, as your servant has heard? O Lord, the God of Israel, I pray you, tell your servant.” The Lord said, “He will come down.”
text.Rotherham: Will the owners of Keilah surrender me into his hand? Will Saul come down, as thy servant hath heard? O Yahweh, God of Israel, I pray thee, tell thy servant. And Yahweh said—He will come down.

text.NRSV: Then David said, “Will the citizens of Keilah surrender me and my men into the hand of Saul?” The Lord said, “They will surrender you.”
text.Rotherham: Then said David, Will the owners of Keilah surrender me and my men, into the hand of Saul? And Yahweh said—They will surrender.

text.NRSV: Then David and his men, who were about six hundred, set out and left Keilah; they moved about among the strongholds.
text.Rotherham: Then arose David and his men, about six hundred, and went forth out of Keilah, and went to and fro, whithersoever they could,—and, unto Saul, it was told that David had escaped out of Keilah, so he forbare to go forth.

text.NRSV: David remained in the strongholds of the wilderness in the hill country of the Wilderness of Ziph. Saul sought him every day, but the Lord did not give him into his hand.
text.Rotherham: Then David abode in the wilderness, in the fortresses, and he abode in the hill country, in the wilderness of Ziph. And Saul sought him continually, but God delivered him not into his hand.

text.NRSV: David was in the Wilderness of Ziph at Horesh when he learned that Saul had come out to seek his life.
text.Rotherham: Then David saw that Saul had come out to seek his life, David, being in the wilderness of Ziph, in the thicket.

text.NRSV: Saul’s son Jonathan set out and came to David at Horesh; there he strengthened his hand in God.
text.Rotherham: So Jonathan, Saul’s son, arose and went unto David in the thicket,—and strengthened his hand in God;

text.NRSV: He said to him, “Do not be afraid; for my father Saul shall not find you; you shall be king over Israel, and I shall be second to you; my father Saul also knows that.”
text.Rotherham: and said unto him,—Do not fear, for the hand of Saul my father shall not find thee, but, thou, shalt become king over Israel, and, I, shall be, next, unto thee, yea and, Saul my father, knoweth this.

text.NRSV: So the two of them made a covenant before the Lord; and David remained at Horesh, and Jonathan went home.
text.Rotherham: And they two solemnised a covenant before Yahweh,—and David remained in the thicket, but, Jonathan, departed to his own house.

text.NRSV: Then the Ziphites went up to Saul at Gibeah, saying, “David is hiding among us in the strongholds of Horesh on the hill of Hachilah, which is south of Jeshimon.
text.Rotherham: Then came up the Ziphites unto Saul in Gibeah, saying,—Is not David hiding himself with us, in the strongholds in the thicket, in the hill of Hachilah, which is on the right of Jeshimon?

text.NRSV: Now, O king, whenever you wish to come down, do so; and our part will be to surrender him into the king’s hand.”
text.Rotherham: Now, therefore, according to all the desire of thy soul O king, to come down, come! and, ours, [will it be] to surrender him into the hand of the king.

text.NRSV: Saul said, “May you be blessed by the Lord; for you have had compassion on me.
text.Rotherham: Then said Saul, Blessed, be ye, of Yahweh,—because ye have taken pity upon me.

text.NRSV: Go and make sure once more; look and see the place where he usually stations himself, and who has seen him there; for I have been told that he is very cunning.
text.Rotherham: Go, I pray you, make ready yet further, and get to know and see his place, where may be his track, who hath seen him there,—for it hath been said unto me, Cunning indeed, is, he!

text.NRSV: Look around and see all the hiding places where he hides, and come back to me with sure information. Then I will go with you; and if he is in the land, I will track him down among all the thousands of Judah.”
text.Rotherham: See, then, and get to know—of all the hiding places, where he hideth himself, and return unto me, for certainty, then will I go with you,—and it shall be, if he is in the land, that I will search him out, through all the thousands of Judah.

text.NRSV: So they set out and went to Ziph ahead of Saul. Now David and his men were in the wilderness of Maon, in the Arabah to the south of Jeshimon.
text.Rotherham: So they arose and departed to Ziph, before Saul,—but, David and his men, were in the wilderness of Maon, in the Arabah, to the right of Jeshimon.

text.NRSV: Saul and his men went to search for him. When David was told, he went down to the rock and stayed in the wilderness of Maon. When Saul heard that, he pursued David into the wilderness of Maon.
text.Rotherham: Then Saul and his men departed, to seek [him] ; but it had been told David, and he had gone down the cliff, and taken up his abode in the wilderness of Maon,—and when Saul had, heard it, he pursued David in the wilderness of Maon.

text.NRSV: Saul went on one side of the mountain, and David and his men on the other side of the mountain. David was hurrying to get away from Saul, while Saul and his men were closing in on David and his men, to capture them.
text.Rotherham: And Saul went on this side of the mountain, and, David and his men, on that side of the mountain,—wherefore David became hurried, to get away from the presence of Saul, but, Saul and his men, were surrounding David and his men, to capture then:

text.NRSV: Then a messenger came to Saul, saying, “Hurry and come; for the Philistines have made a raid on the land.”
text.Rotherham: when, a messenger, came unto Saul, saying,—Haste thee and come! for the Philistines have spread over the land.

text.NRSV: So Saul returned from pursuing David and went to meet the Philistines; therefore that place was called Sela-hammahlekoth.
text.Rotherham: Then returned Saul from pursuing David, and departed to meet the Philistines,—for which cause, they called that place Sela-hammahlekoth ["the Cliff of Separation"].

text.NRSV: David then went up from there and lived in the strongholds of En-gedi.
text.Rotherham: And David went up from thence,—and abode in the strongholds of En-gedi.

text.NRSV: When Saul returned from following the Philistines, he was told, “David is in the wilderness of En-gedi.”
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, when Saul had returned from following the Philistines, that it was told him, saying: Lo! David is in the wilderness of En-gedi.

text.NRSV: Then Saul took three thousand chosen men out of all Israel, and went to look for David and his men in the direction of the Rocks of the Wild Goats.
text.Rotherham: Then Saul took three thousand chosen men, out of all Israel,—and went to seek David and his men, over the face of the rocks of the mountain-goats.

text.NRSV: He came to the sheepfolds beside the road, where there was a cave; and Saul went in to relieve himself. Now David and his men were sitting in the innermost parts of the cave.
text.Rotherham: And he came into the sheep-folds by the way, there, being a cave, which Saul entered, to cover his feet,—David and his men, in the hinder part of the cave, having taken up their abode.

text.NRSV: The men of David said to him, “Here is the day of which the Lord said to you, ‘I will give your enemy into your hand, and you shall do to him as it seems good to you.’” Then David went and stealthily cut off a corner of Saul’s cloak.
text.Rotherham: So David’s men said unto him—Lo! the day of which Yahweh said unto thee—Lo! I am about to deliver up thine enemy, into thy hand: therefore shall thou do unto him, as shall be good in thine eyes. And David arose, and cut off the corner of the robe which belonged to Saul, by stealth.

text.NRSV: Afterward David was stricken to the heart because he had cut off a corner of Saul’s cloak.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass afterwards that David’s heart smote him,—because he had cut off the corner [of the robe] which belonged to Saul.

text.NRSV: He said to his men, “The Lord forbid that I should do this thing to my lord, the Lord’s anointed, to raise my hand against him, for he is the Lord’s anointed.”
text.Rotherham: And he said unto his men—Far be it from me, of Yahweh, that I should do this thing unto my lord, unto the Anointed of Yahweh, to thrust forth my hand against him,—for, the Anointed of Yahweh, is he!

text.NRSV: So David rebuked his men severely and did not permit them to attack Saul. Then Saul got up and left the cave, and went on his way.
text.Rotherham: So David did chide his men with the words, and did not suffer them to rise up against Saul. And, Saul, rose up out of the cave, and went on his way.

text.NRSV: Afterward David also rose up and went out of the cave and called after Saul, “My lord the king!” When Saul looked behind him, David bowed with his face to the ground, and did obeisance.
text.Rotherham: Then David rose up, afterwards, and went forth out of the cave, and cried out after Saul, saying—My lord, O king! And, when Saul looked about behind him, David inclined his face to the earth, and bowed himself down.

text.NRSV: David said to Saul, “Why do you listen to the words of those who say, ‘David seeks to do you harm’?
text.Rotherham: Then said David to Saul, Wherefore shouldst thou hearken unto the words of the sons of earth, saying,—Lo! David is seeking thy hurt?

text.NRSV: This very day your eyes have seen how the Lord gave you into my hand in the cave; and some urged me to kill you, but I spared you. I said, ‘I will not raise my hand against my lord; for he is the Lord’s anointed.’
text.Rotherham: Lo! this day, have thine own eyes seen, how Yahweh had delivered thee up, to-day, into my hand in the cave, and, when one bade me slay thee, I looked with compassion upon thee,—and I said—I will not thrust forth my hand against my lord, for, the Anointed of Yahweh, is he!

text.NRSV: See, my father, see the corner of your cloak in my hand; for by the fact that I cut off the corner of your cloak, and did not kill you, you may know for certain that there is no wrong or treason in my hands. I have not sinned against you, though you are hunting me to take my life.
text.Rotherham: But, my father, see, yea, see, the corner of thy robe in my hand,—for, in that I cut off the corner of thy robe, and yet did not slay thee, know thou, and see, that there is not in my hand either wrong or transgression, neither have I sinned against thee, yet art thou hunting my life, to take it.

text.NRSV: May the Lord judge between me and you! May the Lord avenge me on you; but my hand shall not be against you.
text.Rotherham: Let Yahweh do justice betwixt me and thee, and let Yahweh avenge me of thee,—but, mine own hand, shall not be upon thee.

text.NRSV: As the ancient proverb says, ‘Out of the wicked comes forth wickedness’; but my hand shall not be against you.
text.Rotherham: As saith the proverb of the ancients, From the lawless, proceedeth lawlessness,—mine own hand, therefore shall not be upon thee.

text.NRSV: Against whom has the king of Israel come out? Whom do you pursue? A dead dog? A single flea?
text.Rotherham: After whom, hath the king of Israel come forth? After whom, art thou in pursuit? After a dead dog! after a single flea!

text.NRSV: May the Lord therefore be judge, and give sentence between me and you. May he see and plead my cause, and vindicate me against you.”
text.Rotherham: Therefore shall, Yahweh, become, judge, and do justice betwixt me and thee,—that he may see, and plead my cause, and justly deliver me out of thy hand.

text.NRSV: When David had finished speaking these words to Saul, Saul said, “Is this your voice, my son David?” Saul lifted up his voice and wept.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, when David had made an end of speaking these words unto Saul, that Saul said, Thy voice, is this, my son David? And Saul lifted up his voice, and wept.

text.NRSV: He said to David, “You are more righteous than I; for you have repaid me good, whereas I have repaid you evil.
text.Rotherham: And he said unto David, More righteous, art thou than I, for, thou, hast requited me, good, but, I, have requited thee, evil.

text.NRSV: Today you have explained how you have dealt well with me, in that you did not kill me when the Lord put me into your hand.
text.Rotherham: Thou, then, hast told to-day, how thou hast dealt with me, for good,—how, when Yahweh had surrendered me into thy hand, thou didst not slay me.

text.NRSV: For who has ever found an enemy, and sent the enemy safely away? So may the Lord reward you with good for what you have done to me this day.
text.Rotherham: Yet, when a man findeth his enemy, will he let him get easily away? Yahweh, then, give thee, good, reward for what, this day, thou hast done unto me.

text.NRSV: Now I know that you shall surely be king, and that the kingdom of Israel shall be established in your hand.
text.Rotherham: Now, therefore, lo! I know that thou, shalt indeed become king,—and that the kingdom of Israel, shall be established in thy hand.

text.NRSV: Swear to me therefore by the Lord that you will not cut off my descendants after me, and that you will not wipe out my name from my father’s house.”
text.Rotherham: Now, therefore, swear unto me by Yahweh, that thou wilt not eat off my seed, after me,—and wilt not destroy my name out of the house my father.

text.NRSV: So David swore this oath to Saul. Then Saul went home; but David and his men went up to the stronghold.
text.Rotherham: So David sware unto Saul,—and Saul departed unto his own house, but, David and his men, went up on the stronghold.

text.NRSV: Now Samuel died; and all Israel assembled and mourned for him. They buried him at his home in Ramah. Then David got up and went down to the wilderness of Paran.
text.Rotherham: And Samuel died, and all Israel were gathered together, and made lamentation for him, and buried him within his own house, in Ramah,—and David arose and went down into the wilderness of Maon.

text.NRSV: There was a man in Maon, whose property was in Carmel. The man was very rich; he had three thousand sheep and a thousand goats. He was shearing his sheep in Carmel.
text.Rotherham: Now there was, a man, in Maon, whose cattle were in Carmel, and, the man, was exceeding great, and, he, had three thousand sheep, and a thousand goats,—and so it was, that he was shearing his sheep in Carmel.

text.NRSV: Now the name of the man was Nabal, and the name of his wife Abigail. The woman was clever and beautiful, but the man was surly and mean; he was a Calebite.
text.Rotherham: Now, the name of the man, was Nabal, and, the name of his wife, Abigail,—and, the woman, was of good understanding, and of beautiful figure,—but, the man, was unfeeling and of evil practices, and he was a Calebite.

text.NRSV: David heard in the wilderness that Nabal was shearing his sheep.
text.Rotherham: So then David heard in the wilderness,—that Nabal was shearing his sheep.

text.NRSV: So David sent ten young men; and David said to the young men, “Go up to Carmel, and go to Nabal, and greet him in my name.
text.Rotherham: David, therefore, sent ten young men,—and David said unto the young men,—Go up to Carmel, and enter in unto Nabal, and ye shall ask him, in my name, of his welfare;

text.NRSV: Thus you shall salute him, ‘Peace be to you, and peace be to your house, and peace be to all that you have.
text.Rotherham: and say thus—Long life to thee! Mayest, thou, prosper, And, thy household, prosper, And, all that thou hast, prosper!

text.NRSV: I hear that you have shearers; now your shepherds have been with us, and we did them no harm, and they missed nothing, all the time they were in Carmel.
text.Rotherham: Now, therefore, have I heard that thou hast shearers,—Well, the shepherds that thou hast, have been with us, we reproached them not, neither missed they, anything, all the days they were in Carmel.

text.NRSV: Ask your young men, and they will tell you. Therefore let the young men find favor in your sight; for we come on a feast day. Please give whatever you have at hand to your servants and to your son David.’”
text.Rotherham: Ask thy young men, that they may tell thee, that so my young men may find favour in thine eyes, for, on a good day, have we come! Give, I pray thee, what thy hand findeth, to thy servants, and to thy son, to David!

text.NRSV: When David’s young men came, they said all this to Nabal in the name of David; and then they waited.
text.Rotherham: So David’s young men came, and spake unto Nabal, according to all these words, in the name of David,—and waited.

text.NRSV: But Nabal answered David’s servants, “Who is David? Who is the son of Jesse? There are many servants today who are breaking away from their masters.
text.Rotherham: Then Nabal answered the servants of David, and said, Who is David? And who is the son of Jesse? Nowadays, many are the servants that have broken away, every one from the presence of his lord:

text.NRSV: Shall I take my bread and my water and the meat that I have butchered for my shearers, and give it to men who come from I do not know where?”
text.Rotherham: Shall I, then, take my bread, and my wine, and my slain beasts, that I have slaughtered for my shearers,—and give unto men of whom I know not whence they are?

text.NRSV: So David’s young men turned away, and came back, and told him all this.
text.Rotherham: So the young men of David turned away for their journey,—and came back, and drew near, and told him, according to all these words.

text.NRSV: David said to his men, “Every man strap on his sword!” And every one of them strapped on his sword; David also strapped on his sword; and about four hundred men went up after David, while two hundred remained with the baggage.
text.Rotherham: Then said David unto his men—Gird on you every man his sword. And they girded on them, every man his sword, and, David also, girded on his sword,—and there went up after David—about four hundred men, and, two hundred, abode by the stores.

text.NRSV: But one of the young men told Abigail, Nabal’s wife, “David sent messengers out of the wilderness to salute our master; and he shouted insults at them.
text.Rotherham: But a certain one of her young men told, Abigail, Nabal’s wife, saying,—Lo! David sent messengers out of the wilderness to bless our lord, and he treated them with contempt.

text.NRSV: Yet the men were very good to us, and we suffered no harm, and we never missed anything when we were in the fields as long as we were with them;
text.Rotherham: But, the men, were exceeding good to us,—and we were not harmed, neither missed we, anything, all the days we went to and fro with them, while we were in the fields:

text.NRSV: they were a wall to us both by night and by day, all the while we were with them keeping the sheep.
text.Rotherham: A wall, became they unto us, both by night and by day, all the days we were with them, tending the flock.

text.NRSV: Now therefore know this and consider what you should do; for evil has been decided against our master and against all his house; he is so ill-natured that no one can speak to him.”
text.Rotherham: Now, therefore, know thou, and see, what thou canst do, for mischief is determined against our lord, and against all his household,—but, he, is such an abandoned man, that one cannot speak unto him.

text.NRSV: Then Abigail hurried and took two hundred loaves, two skins of wine, five sheep ready dressed, five measures of parched grain, one hundred clusters of raisins, and two hundred cakes of figs. She loaded them on donkeys
text.Rotherham: Then Abigail hastened—and took two hundred loaves, and two skins of wine, and five sheep made ready, and five measures of parched corn, and a hundred cakes of raisins, and two hundred cakes of figs,—and put them on the asses.

text.NRSV: and said to her young men, “Go on ahead of me; I am coming after you.” But she did not tell her husband Nabal.
text.Rotherham: And she said to her young men—Pass on before me, behold me coming after you; but, to her husband, Nabal, told she nothing.

text.NRSV: While she was on the donkey and coming down under cover of the mountain, David and his men came down toward her; and she met them.
text.Rotherham: And so it was, as she was riding on her ass, and descending within the covert of the hill, lo! David and his men, descending over against her,—so she met them.

text.NRSV: Now David had said, “Surely it was for nothing that I protected all that this fellow has in the wilderness, so that nothing was missed of all that belonged to him; but he has returned me evil for good.
text.Rotherham: Now, David, had said—Surely, in vain, did I guard all that pertained to this man in the wilderness, so that there was, nothing, missed of all that he had,—seeing he hath returned to me evil for good.

text.NRSV: God do so to David and more also, if by morning I leave so much as one male of all who belong to him.”
text.Rotherham: So may God do to David and so may he add, if I leave remaining, of all that he hath, until the morning,—so much as a little boy.

text.NRSV: When Abigail saw David, she hurried and dismounted from the donkey and fell before David on her face, bowing to the ground.
text.Rotherham: And, when Abigail saw David, she hastened, and alighted from off her ass,—and fell down before David upon her face, and prostrated herself on the ground;

text.NRSV: She fell at his feet and said, “Upon me alone, my lord, be the guilt; please let your servant speak in your ears, and hear the words of your servant.
text.Rotherham: yea she fell at his feet, and said—On me, even me, my lord, be the transgression,—But, I pray thee, let thy handmaid speak in thine ears, and hear thou the words of thy handmaid.

text.NRSV: My lord, do not take seriously this ill-natured fellow, Nabal; for as his name is, so is he; Nabal is his name, and folly is with him; but I, your servant, did not see the young men of my lord, whom you sent.
text.Rotherham: Let it not be, I pray thee, that my lord regard this abandoned man—Nabal [="Base"]; For, as his name is, so, is he. Nabal, is his name, and, baseness, is with him,—But, I, thy handmaid, saw not the young men of my lord, whom thou didst send.

text.NRSV: Now then, my lord, as the Lord lives and as you yourself live, since the Lord has restrained you from bloodguilt and from avenging yourself with your own hand, now let your enemies and those who seek to do evil to my lord be like Nabal.
text.Rotherham: Now, therefore, my lord—By the life of Yahweh, and by the life of thine own soul,—seeing Yahweh hath withholden thee from coming in with bloodshed, and from saving thyself, with thine own hand, now, therefore, like Nabal, be thine enemies, and they who are making search for my lord, wrongfully.

text.NRSV: And now let this present that your servant has brought to my lord be given to the young men who follow my lord.
text.Rotherham: Now, therefore, this blessing which thy maid-servant hath brought to my lord, let it even be given unto the young men who are going to and fro at the feet of my lord.

text.NRSV: Please forgive the trespass of your servant; for the Lord will certainly make my lord a sure house, because my lord is fighting the battles of the Lord; and evil shall not be found in you so long as you live.
text.Rotherham: Forgive, I pray thee, the trespass of thy handmaid,—for Yahweh, will certainly make, for my lord an assured house, for, the battles of Yahweh, is my lord fighting, and, wrong, shall not be found in thee, all thy days;

text.NRSV: If men should rise up to pursue you and to seek your life, the life of my lord shall be bound in the bundle of the living under the care of the Lord your God; but the lives of your enemies he shall sling out as from the hollow of a sling.
text.Rotherham: yea, though there hath arisen a sun of earth to pursue thee, and to seek thy life, yet shall the life of my lord be bound up in the bundle of the living, with Yahweh thy God, but, as for the life of thine enemies, he shall sling it out with the middle of the hollow of the sling.

text.NRSV: When the Lord has done to my lord according to all the good that he has spoken concerning you, and has appointed you prince over Israel,
text.Rotherham: And it shall be, when Yahweh shall do for my lord, according to all the good that he hath spoken concerning thee,—and shall put thee in charge as leader over Israel,

text.NRSV: my lord shall have no cause of grief, or pangs of conscience, for having shed blood without cause or for my lord having avenged himself. When the Lord has dealt well with my lord, then remember your servant.”
text.Rotherham: then shall this not become to thee a staggering and stumbling of heart, unto my lord—that thou didst either shed blood without need, or that the hand of my lord saved himself. And, when Yahweh hath dealt well with my lord, then remember thou thy handmaid.

text.NRSV: David said to Abigail, “Blessed be the Lord, the God of Israel, who sent you to meet me today!
text.Rotherham: Then said David unto Abigail,—Blessed, be Yahweh, the God of Israel, who hath sent thee this day, to meet me;

text.NRSV: Blessed be your good sense, and blessed be you, who kept me today from bloodguilt and from avenging myself by my own hand!
text.Rotherham: and, blessed, be thy discreet judgment, and, blessed, be thou thyself,—who hast kept me, this day, from coming in with bloodshed, and from saving myself, with mine own hand.

text.NRSV: For as the Lord the God of Israel lives, who has restrained me from hurting you, unless you had hurried and come to meet me, truly by morning there had not been left to Nabal so much as one male.”
text.Rotherham: Nevertheless, by the life of Yahweh, God of Israel, who hath restrained me from harming, thee, surely, except thou hadst hastened and come to meet me, there had not been left unto Nabal, by the light of the morning, so much as a little boy.

text.NRSV: Then David received from her hand what she had brought him; he said to her, “Go up to your house in peace; see, I have heeded your voice, and have granted your petition.”
text.Rotherham: So David received at her hand, that which she had brought him,—and, unto herself, he said—Go up, in peace, unto thy house, see! I have hearkened unto thy voice, and accepted thy person.

text.NRSV: Abigail came to Nabal; he was holding a feast in his house, like the feast of a king. Nabal’s heart was merry within him, for he was very drunk; so she told him nothing at all until the morning light.
text.Rotherham: And, when Abigail came unto Nabal, lo! he, had a banquet in his house, like the banquet of a king, and, the heart of Nabal, was glad accordingly, he having drunk deeply,—so she told him nothing—less or more, until the light of the morning.

text.NRSV: In the morning, when the wine had gone out of Nabal, his wife told him these things, and his heart died within him; he became like a stone.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, in the morning, when the wine had gone out of Nabal, and his wife told him these things, that his heart died within him, and he became like a stone.

text.NRSV: About ten days later the Lord struck Nabal, and he died.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, in about ten days, that Yahweh smote Nabal, that he died.

text.NRSV: When David heard that Nabal was dead, he said, “Blessed be the Lord who has judged the case of my insult at the hand of Nabal, and has restrained his servant from evil; the Lord has returned the evildoing of Nabal upon his own head.” Then David sent word to Abigail, to make her his wife.
text.Rotherham: And, when David heard that Nabal was dead, he said—Blessed, be Yahweh, who hath maintained the plea of my reproach, at the hand of Nabal, and hath restrained, his servant, from wrong, yea, the wrong of Nabal, hath Yahweh turned back on his own head. Then sent David, and spake with Abigail, to take her to himself wife.

text.NRSV: When David’s servants came to Abigail at Carmel, they said to her, “David has sent us to you to make you his wife.”
text.Rotherham: And the servants of David came unto Abigail the Carmelitess,—and spake unto her, saying, David, hath sent us unto thee, to take thee to himself to wife.

text.NRSV: She rose and bowed down, with her face to the ground, and said, “Your servant is a slave to wash the feet of the servants of my lord.”
text.Rotherham: And she arose, and bowed herself down with her face to the earth,—and said—Lo! thy handmaid, as serving-woman to bathe the feet of the servants of my lord.

text.NRSV: Abigail hurried and rose and mounted a donkey; her five maids attended her. She followed the messengers of David and became his wife.
text.Rotherham: And Abigail hastened, and mounted, and rode upon her ass, with her five damsels that used to journey at her feet,—and she went her way after the messengers of David, and became his wife.

text.NRSV: David also married Ahinoam of Jezreel; both of them also became his wives.
text.Rotherham: David took, Ahinoam also, out of Jezreel,—and, they two, became his wives.

text.NRSV: Saul had given his daughter Michal, David’s wife, to Palti son of Laish, who was from Gallim.
text.Rotherham: Saul, indeed, had given his daughter Michal, David’s wife,—to Palti, sun of Laish, who was of Gallim.

text.NRSV: Then the Ziphites came to Saul at Gibeah, saying, “David is in hiding on the hill of Hachilah, which is opposite Jeshimon.”
text.Rotherham: And the Ziphites came unto Saul in Gibeah, saying,—Is not David hiding himself in the hill of Hachilah, overlooking Jeshimon?
